top_findings.summary: The skill requests the ability to run arbitrary shell commands (via KillShell and BashOutput) and terminate processes, which is excessive for a code analysis tool focused on static analysis (tracing execution paths, mapping architecture, understanding patterns). The stated primary purpose can be achieved using read-only tools (Glob, Grep, LS, Read, NotebookRead) and web tools for documentation (WebFetch, WebSearch) without requiring shell execution capabilities, which pose a security risk if used for unintended actions on the user's system.
